round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Acknowledged other agents' findings (infrastructure, landscape) to reinforce the linguistic evidence, but the core reasoning remained static.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Reinforced the initial assessment by citing the council's findings on infrastructure and agriculture, but did not introduce new visual analysis.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Synthesized cross-domain evidence (linguistic, architectural) to support the botanical observation, but the botanical evidence itself was unchanged.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Integrated findings on vehicle models and infrastructure to support the regulatory analysis, but the visual evidence cited was identical to R1.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Combined specific visual cues (poles, shutters) with other agents' findings to strengthen the conclusion, but the visual identification remained the same.

role_leakage_notes.linguistic: Referenced 'council's findings on concrete utility poles' (regulatory/meta) and 'arid landscape' (landscape) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.landscape: Referenced 'road infrastructure noted by the council' (regulatory) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Referenced 'North American vehicle models' (meta) and 'concrete utility poles' (meta) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Referenced 'Linguistic Agent's identification' and 'Landscape Agent's observation' in R2.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge provided a clear, evidence-based synthesis. It explicitly listed the visual evidence (signage, infrastructure, vegetation) and correctly mapped it to the ground truth (Mexico). It effectively summarized the consensus across all agents and correctly downgraded alternative candidates based on the lack of supporting evidence.
